name: Montage: Beat 6a — The Greenweald: Ashen Woods Route

pages.name: Beat 6a — The Greenweald: Ashen Woods Route

text.content:
Beat 6a — The Greenweald: Ashen Woods Route

Act: Act 1 | Type: Montage Scene

Overview

The Long Road Through the Ashen Woods

Level: 2 | Beat: 6 | Location: Interior Belt → Ashen Woods → Green Heart

~160 miles via the Ashen Woods. ~9 days. The journey is broken into two montage test rounds separated by combat encounters and narrative interludes.

Difficulty: Hard (adjusted for 4 heroes + Lew as NPC companion) | Rounds: 2

Lew's Contribution: Assists one PC's test per round (Intuition +4 or Reason +2). His navigation is automatic—the PCs cannot get lost while his eye is active. They can be delayed, ambushed, or worn down, but not lost.

Outcomes

  • Total Success (before failure limit): Arrive at the Green Heart's boundary intact and healthy. Earn 2 Milestones. Grafvitnir's network is behind them—the dead woods disrupted tracking.
  • Partial Success (failure limit hit, 2+ more successes than failures): Arrive at cost—choose: rations critically low (−1 Might tests until fed properly), one PC rattled (−2 Presence tests until extended rest), or next Grafvitnir Pressure Check triggers at 4+. Earn 1 Victory.
  • Total Failure: Arrive exhausted and compromised. All PCs lose 1 Hit Die. Grafvitnir's agents are ahead of them—Pale Maw agents waiting near the Green Heart boundary. No Milestones. Director gains 4 Malice. Lew is injured (−2 speed until healed).

Running This Scene

This is a narrative montage — a series of challenges the party faces as a group. Rather than a single roll, play this out as a sequence of scenes where each character contributes.

GM Guidance

  • Go around the table and ask each player how their character is helping.
  • Call for ability checks as appropriate — suggested modified [Resolute←15] (hard).
  • Let creative approaches succeed with good roleplaying, even without a roll.
  • Use the complications below as prompts — not every one needs to come up.
  • The goal is collaborative storytelling, not a pass/fail test.

Relevant Skills:

  • Strong
  • Quick or Discreet
  • Cunning or Cunning
  • Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant
  • Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive

Complications & Challenges

Use these as dramatic beats during the montage. Present them as situations the party must navigate together.

Round 1: The Dead Farms (Days 1–3)

Navigate the Failing Road Network (Cunning or Cunning, modified [Resolute←15])

Collapsed bridges, missing markers, roads that end in frozen bogs. Plot a route through the decay—every wrong turn costs precious daylight in a landscape where nights kill.

Scavenge Supplies from Abandoned Crofts (Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant, modified [Resolute←15])

The farmsteads aren't empty—they're abandoned. Find preserved food, firewood, rope, and tools. modified [Resolute←14] to find something useful without triggering a trap or structural collapse.

Endure the Cold and Manage Rationing (Strong, modified [Resolute←15])

The Interior Belt is colder than the coast. The wind cuts through everything. Frostbite is a real threat for the unprepared, and the supplies must stretch nine days through this wasteland.

Avoid Refugee Columns (Presence / Agility)

Families fleeing southwest toward Hrafnborg clog the road. The PCs are heading southeast when everyone flees southwest—dangerous questions will be asked about their direction and their motives.

Cross a Frozen River (Quick or Discreet, modified [Resolute←15])

The ice looks solid. It isn't. The current beneath is fast and cold enough to kill in minutes. The wrong step means a plunge into black water with full packs and no rope near enough to matter.

Keep Morale Up at Camp (Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive, modified [Resolute←15])

The landscape is grey and crushing. Three days of charcoal silence and dead farms erodes the spirit. The PCs need to keep each other (and Lew) from despair—stories, songs, small comforts at a fire that doesn't feel like enough.

Escalation

If the scene needs more tension, introduce these additional complications:

Round 2: Into the Ashen Woods (Days 4–9)

Phase 1 — The Pathless Interior (Days 4–6)

Navigate the Pathless Interior (Reason / Intuition)

No roads, no landmarks—just endless charcoal pillars. Lew's eye helps, but the PCs must find passable routes through deadfall and ash-choked ravines. The woods shift: fallen trunks block yesterday's path.

Resist the Silence (Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive, modified [Resolute←15])

The Ashen Woods are psychologically oppressive. The silence gets inside your head. Characters who fail become rattled (−2 Presence tests) until the next rest. The absence of sound becomes a sound in itself.

Cross the Char-Gorge (Agility / Might)

A ravine splits the forest floor—a riverbed that went dry after the fire. The sides are crumbling charcoal and exposed root networks. The bottom is 30 feet down and full of ash. Rope, climbing, or creative solutions needed.

Find Clean Water (Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant, modified [Resolute←15])

All surface water in the Ashen Woods is tainted—grey, ashy, sometimes actively corrosive. Finding a clean spring or knowing how to filter the water is critical to survival. Without it, the march becomes a death march.

Spot Grafvitnir's Agents (Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant, modified [Resolute←15])

Silver-eyed carrion birds circle at the edge of vision. A figure on a far ridge moves against the wind. If noticed, the PCs realise they've been watched since Stairwatch. If not noticed, the Grafvitnir Pressure Check result worsens.

Camp Safely in the Burn Zone (Cunning or Cunning, modified [Resolute←15])

The charcoal trunks are unstable. Setting camp under one is asking for it to collapse. Finding stable ground, clearing a firebreak, and keeping watch in a landscape where everything is the same colour of grey is its own hazard.

Phase 2 — Approaching the Heart (Days 7–9)

Follow the Root-Network to the Green Heart (Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant, modified [Resolute←15])

The living roots beneath the ash are the map now. They branch, split, and sometimes loop back. Lew's eye helps, but the PCs must read the root-pattern to find the most direct path toward the Green Heart.

Navigate a Char-Storm (Might / Agility)

Wind picks up without warning. The dead trunks shed charcoal in great sheets—a black blizzard that blinds, chokes, and disorients. Shelter or endurance: take your pick before someone gets separated.

Treat Wounds and Illness from the Ash (Cunning or Cunning, modified [Resolute←15])

Days of charcoal dust have taken a toll: coughing, irritated eyes, cracked skin. Proper treatment (herb lore, clean water, bandages) prevents a condition from setting in permanently before they reach the Heart.

Evade a Blighted Predator Pack (Agility / Intuition)

A pack of Rot-Beasts stalks the party from a distance—too many to fight (6+). The PCs must circle around, move downwind, or find defensible ground and wait them out before continuing.

Maintain Supply Discipline (Cunning or Cunning, modified [Resolute←15])

Nine days is a long time. If supplies weren't managed carefully in earlier rounds, the party is hungry, cold, and weakening at exactly the wrong moment. A final rationing check determines arrival condition.

Calm Lew's Fever (Presence / Intuition)

Lew's eye burns hotter as they get closer to the Heart. He develops a low fever, talks in his sleep, and occasionally turns in circles muttering about "the shape in the roots." He needs reassurance and rest—or at least a kind word to keep him functional.

Outcomes

Based on how the party performed, use the following as a guide:

Round 1 Interlude: The Last Farm

The last farmstead before the Ashen Woods is a proper stone longhouse, still mostly intact. The door is barred from the outside. Inside: a family's belongings, neatly packed, and a note on the table: "We go southwest. May the Ember at Hrafnborg still burn. If you read this and the fire is out—God help you. God help us all."

The Ever-Ember niche is cold. The ash is weeks old.

If the PCs search: preserved food (1 day's rations), a good hand-axe, and a child's doll left on a bed with its face turned toward the southeast. Watching something.

Round 2 Interlude — The Green Thread

Midway through the deep woods, Lew stops and brushes ash from the ground. Beneath the char: a living root, thick as a man's arm, running through dead soil—alive. Pale green bark, sap beading, warm to the touch. It runs southeast—toward the Green Heart. "That's not a tree root. That's a taproot. Something at the centre of these woods is still alive."

Round 2 Interlude — The Singing Stone

A clearing where a single upright boulder of dark granite stands, taller than a man. It hums—not audibly, but felt in teeth and fingertips. Ash has been pushed aside by the vibration, revealing bare earth and dozens of roots converging on the stone like spokes on a wheel. Lew presses his palm against it. His eye flares bright amber: "The Tree. This stone is sitting on one of the Tree's roots. A deep one."

Any PC touching the stone feels genuine warmth, like standing beside a great fire—for one moment, then it fades. This is a root-node: a point where Yggdrasil's deep root network surfaces.

Total Success

Arrive at the Green Heart's boundary intact and healthy. Earn 2 Milestones. Grafvitnir's network is behind them—the dead woods disrupted tracking. The party has supplies, composure, and time to prepare.

Partial Success

Arrive at cost. Choose one: rations critically low (−1 Might tests until fed properly); one PC rattled (−2 Presence tests until a extended rest); or next Grafvitnir Pressure Check triggers automatically at 4+. Earn 1 Victory.

Total Failure

Arrive exhausted and compromised. All PCs lose 1 Hit Die. Grafvitnir's agents are ahead—Pale Maw operatives waiting near the Green Heart boundary. No Milestones. Director gains 4 Malice. Lew is injured (twisted ankle; −2 speed until healed).
